title: Silent Spring legacy
script_summary: Explore the nuanced legacy of Silent Spring and its impact on environmental science.

segments.voiceover: What if a book changed how we see nature forever?
In 1962, Rachel Carson's 'Silent Spring' revealed the dangers of pesticides.
But not all scientists agreed. Controversy sparked heated debates.
Some argued Carson exaggerated risks, labeling her a fearmonger.
Yet, her work led to the U.S. banning DDT and sparked the environmental movement.
But here's the catch: many questions still linger.
Curious about the lasting impact? Hit follow for more science insights!
